quote_verbatim: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.
paraphrase_en: High-risk AI systems must be designed for effective human oversight while in use, including appropriate human-machine interface tools. A high-risk agentic system executing consequential actions autonomously would need architecturally real oversight, not nominal review.
confidence_rationale: Text verified verbatim this session via the Commission's AI Act Service Desk (Tier 2 official mirror of the Tier 1 EUR-Lex text). Confidence capped at medium per SPEC §22: a known adopted-but-unpublished amendment (2026 omnibus) defers this provision's application date from 2026-08-02 to 2027-12-02 (Annex III).

epistemic_blocks.text_md: Article 14(1) AI Act requires high-risk AI systems to be designed and developed so that natural persons can effectively oversee them during use.

On 2026-06-29 the Council gave final approval to the omnibus amendment deferring Annex III high-risk obligations (which include Art 14 for such systems) to 2027-12-02; the amending act awaited Official Journal publication as of 2026-07-12.

citations.title: AI: Council gives final green light to simplify and streamline rules

epistemic_blocks.text_md: For an agentic system that is (or is embedded in) a high-risk system, Art 14 reaches autonomous state-changing execution (C5) and multi-step planning (C7): oversight must be effective during use, which post-hoc review alone may not satisfy at anchor A=3+.

Operators of high-risk-class agentic systems could place the approval gate at the tool-execution boundary (control class M1) and instrument it with the §18.5 action log so oversight is demonstrably effective, not nominal.

capability_map.rationale: Oversight duty attaches to state-changing execution in high-risk contexts.
Effective oversight during use constrains autonomous multi-step operation.
High-risk classification frequently coincides with consequential decision-making.

quote_verbatim: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system
paraphrase_en: AI systems that interact directly with people must be designed so users know they are dealing with AI, unless that is obvious to a reasonably informed person in context. Applies from 2026-08-02 and was NOT deferred by the 2026 omnibus.
confidence_rationale: First-clause text verified verbatim this session (Commission AI Act Service Desk; quote truncated to ≤50 words per §21.3 — the full sentence adds a reasonableness qualifier). Not-deferred status corroborated by the Council press release (fetch blocked, 403) and multiple independent secondary reports; capped medium until the omnibus text is checked in the OJ.

epistemic_blocks.text_md: Article 50(1) AI Act requires providers to design direct-interaction AI systems so the persons concerned are informed they are interacting with an AI system (subject to an obviousness qualifier).

Art 50 transparency obligations apply from 2026-08-02; the 2026 omnibus deferral of high-risk deadlines did not defer Art 50(1) (Art 50(2) watermarking gains a limited extension to 2026-12-02 for systems already on the market).

citations.title: AI: Council gives final green light to simplify and streamline rules

epistemic_blocks.text_md: This reaches customer-facing agents (C1 generation + C6 external communication) directly: a support or companion agent interacting with EU users needs AI disclosure from 2026-08-02 — three weeks from this record's last_verified date — regardless of the high-risk deferral.
Operators of user-facing agents could treat AI-disclosure (control class M8) as a launch-blocking control for EU deployments now, since this obligation's date held while the high-risk dates moved.

capability_map.rationale: Disclosure duty attaches to systems generating interaction with humans.
Direct interaction with natural persons is the operative trigger.

risk_map.rationale: Provision directly targets deceptive interaction (humans unaware they face AI).

paraphrase_en: Data subjects have the right not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, that produce legal effects or similarly significantly affect them — the core EU constraint on fully automated consequential decisions by agents (C8).
confidence_rationale: Paraphrase-only record: instrument metadata verified against the EUR-Lex CELEX record this session, but Art 22's verbatim wording was not re-fetched; per the project's verification discipline no quote is included and confidence is capped at medium until the text is re-verified in a review pass.

epistemic_blocks.text_md: GDPR Article 22(1) grants the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects or similarly significantly affects the data subject.

An agent that finalizes consequential outcomes without independent human judgment (C8 at intensity ≥2) operates in Art 22 territory; a genuine human-in-the-loop (M1) is the classic path out of 'solely automated'.

Operators of consequential-decision agents in the EU could route Art-22-relevant outcomes through a meaningful human decision point (M1) and log the human's role (M3) as evidence the decision was not solely automated.

capability_map.rationale: Art 22 is the paradigmatic consequential-automated-decision provision.

risk_map.rationale: Provision targets consequential-decision harm from solely automated processing.

pin_cite: (developer documentation duty — pin cite to section pending full-text pass)
paraphrase_en: From 2027-01-01, developers of automated decision-making technology used in consequential decisions must provide technical documentation to deployers — a disclosure-centered duty replacing the 2024 act's duty-of-care and impact-assessment model.
confidence_rationale: Verified against the official bill page (Tier 1) this session at summary level; the full enrolled text has not yet been read section-by-section, so the pin cite is deferred and confidence capped medium until the Pass 2 full-text pass.

epistemic_blocks.text_md: SB26-189 (signed 2026-05-14) requires ADMT developers to provide technical documentation to deployers, with compliance obligations beginning 2027-01-01.

An agent that finalizes consequential outcomes (C8 at intensity ≥2) in employment, housing, lending, insurance, healthcare, education, or benefits contexts in Colorado will sit inside this ADMT framework once applicable; the deferred-until-2027 date makes this a near_term_hit candidate, not a binding_hit, for assessments dated mid-2026.

Operators of consequential-decision agents deployed in Colorado could stand up the capability-profile documentation and data-flow map controls (documentation control class) now, since the 2027 duty is disclosure-shaped rather than risk-management-shaped.

capability_map.rationale: The statute's trigger is ADMT use in consequential decisions.

pin_cite: 定义条款 (article number pending full-text pass)
quote_verbatim: 在生成合成内容或者交互场景界面中添加的，以文字、声音、图形等方式呈现并可以被用户明显感知到的标识。
paraphrase_en: Defines an 'explicit label' (显式标识): a label added to generated synthetic content or the interaction interface, presented as text, sound, or graphics, clearly perceptible to users. A companion definition covers implicit labels (隐式标识) embedded technically in file data. Applies to all AI-generated text, images, audio, and video since 2025-09-01.
confidence_rationale: Chinese original quoted verbatim from the Tier 1 CAC text fetched this session (authoritative language per §6.4). Capped medium because the English paraphrase is the analyst's own translation (§7.5 cap) and the article number awaits the full-text pass.

epistemic_blocks.text_md: The Measures define explicit labels (显式标识) as user-perceptible text/sound/graphic marks on generated synthetic content or its interaction interface, and implicit labels (隐式标识) as technically embedded marks in content file data.

citations.title: 人工智能生成合成内容标识办法

epistemic_blocks.text_md: A generative agent serving CN users (C1) that posts publicly (C6) must emit BOTH label layers: explicit marks in the rendered content/UI and implicit marks in file metadata — a concrete engineering requirement on the content pipeline, currently applicable (binding_hit driver for CN assessments of C1≥2 agents).

Operators of content-generating agents in CN could implement the synthetic-content watermark/label-injection control (M8 class) at the rendering layer AND the file-metadata layer, and log label application per output (M3) as compliance evidence.

capability_map.rationale: Labeling duty attaches to generated synthetic content.
Interaction-interface and publicly distributed content are expressly covered.

risk_map.rationale: Part of the CN content-governance stack.
